sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Vanguard (NYSE: ARW) reports 3.22M-share, 6.3% stake in Arrow Electronics
feed.summary: Vanguard Portfolio Management has reported a beneficial ownership of 3,222,548 shares, representing a 6.3% stake in Arrow Electronics (ARW), as disclosed in a Schedule 13G SEC filing. This filing indicates a passive investment intent, with Vanguard exercising sole dispositive power over all reported shares but sole voting power for only 24,046 shares. The holdings are distributed across various Vanguard funds and managed accounts.

feed.title: AstraZeneca beats profit expectations, keeps annual outlook unchanged
feed.summary: AstraZeneca surpassed first-quarter sales and profit estimates, largely due to strong demand for its cancer drugs. The company reaffirmed its 2026 financial outlook, projecting low double-digit percentage growth in core earnings per share and mid-to-high single-digit revenue growth at constant currencies. CEO Pascal Soriot continues to lead the company toward its goal of $80 billion in annual sales by 2030, expanding its global presence despite geopolitical complexities.

feed.title: PTC (PTC) Expected to Announce Quarterly Earnings on Wednesday
feed.summary: PTC (NASDAQ:PTC) is scheduled to release its Q2 2026 earnings on Wednesday, May 6th, with analysts forecasting earnings of $2.11 per share and revenue of $715.45 million. The company exceeded expectations in its previous quarter, reporting $1.92 EPS against an anticipated $1.59, and its revenue increased 21.4% year-over-year. Wall Street maintains a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with a target price of $182.08 for PTC.

feed.title: MetLife (MET) Projected to Post Earnings on Wednesday
feed.summary: MetLife (MET) is expected to announce its Q1 2026 earnings after market close on Wednesday, May 6th, with analysts projecting $2.20 EPS and $19.29 billion in revenue. The company previously beat estimates in Q4 2025 with $2.49 EPS and $52.44 billion in revenue. Currently, the stock has a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating and a target price of $93.38.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley launches first major Wall Street spot Bitcoin ETF
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has launched its spot Bitcoin ETF (MSBT), marking it as the first major Wall Street bank to offer such a product. The MSBT carries a 0.14% fee, aiming to attract institutional capital, but the market's expectation for Bitcoin reaching $80,000 in April has dropped, indicating that traders believe broader headwinds beyond the ETF's launch are influencing Bitcoin's price. The article suggests that future net inflow data for MSBT and regulatory shifts will be key factors to watch for Bitcoin's price movements.

feed.title: Colgate, in partnership with Hong Kong Dental Association, launches TOTAL Periodontal Health Lab
feed.summary: Colgate, in partnership with the Hong Kong Dental Association, has launched the "TOTAL Periodontal Health Lab" to raise public awareness about gum disease prevention. The initiative, supported by PARKnSHOP and Watsons, includes Hong Kong's first community-wide oral health survey aiming to collect 5,000 data samples to inform future oral care strategies and offers free dental consultations and interactive educational activities. It extends Colgate's "Bright Smiles, Bright Futures" mission by donating toothpaste for every smile collected for charity.

feed.title: Vanguard Portfolio Management (AROC) discloses 6.33% ownership, 11.1M shares
feed.summary: Vanguard Portfolio Management has disclosed a 6.33% ownership stake in Archrock Inc. (AROC), amounting to 11,095,912 shares of common stock as of March 31, 2026. The filing, a Schedule 13G, indicates that Vanguard holds sole dispositive power over all these shares but only sole voting power for 158,065 shares, signifying a passive investment. This aggregated holding reflects securities managed across Vanguard Portfolio Management LLC and its affiliates.

feed.title: American Eagle (NYSE: AEO) awards 915 dividend rights to brand president
feed.summary: American Eagle (NYSE: AEO) Global Brand President, Jennifer M. Foyle, was granted 915 dividend equivalent rights tied to previously awarded restricted stock units (RSUs). This grant increases her total reported dividend equivalent rights to 3,666. These rights are the economic equivalent of one share of common stock per right and will vest proportionately with the related RSUs.

feed.title: DRONE STRATEGY: AEROVIRONMENT, VOLATUS AEROSPACE, OR DRONESHIELD – WHICH ONE WILL TAKE OFF AGAIN?
feed.summary: This article analyzes the investment prospects of three drone-related companies: AeroVironment, Volatus Aerospace, and DroneShield, amidst a cooling market for drone stocks. It examines their respective business models—offensive drones, versatile ISR and logistics drones, and counter-drone defense systems—and their financial valuations. The author questions which company is best positioned for future growth, especially given current high valuations and the shift from euphoria to a more discerning market.

feed.title: Stryker (SYK) Q1 Earnings: What To Expect
feed.summary: Stryker (NYSE:SYK) is anticipated to report its Q1 earnings this Thursday, with market expectations for an 8% year-on-year revenue growth. The company has a history of exceeding Wall Street's expectations and analysts have reconfirmed their estimates. Peers in the medical devices & supplies segment have shown mixed results, while investor sentiment in the sector has been positive overall.

feed.title: Gold Shrugs Off the Oil Crisis – Who Are the Winners? Barrick Mining, DRC Gold, and Occidental Petroleum in the Spotlight
feed.summary: Despite escalating Middle East conflicts and rising gas prices, gold remains stable, highlighting its role as a crisis hedge. The article discusses Barrick Mining's diversification across gold and copper, DRC Gold's promising East African gold projects, and Occidental Petroleum’s unexpected gains from oil market volatility. It highlights these companies as potential winners in the current volatile economic climate, suggesting they offer significant investment opportunities.

feed.title: Is It Too Late To Consider Axos Financial (AX) After Its Strong 1-Year Share Price Rally?
feed.summary: Axos Financial (AX) has seen a significant 54.7% share price rally over the last year, prompting questions about its current valuation. According to Simply Wall St's Excess Returns model, the stock appears significantly undervalued by 62.6%, with an intrinsic value estimate of $264.41 per share compared to its current trading price of $98.89. The company's P/E ratio of 12.27x also suggests it is undervalued when compared to Simply Wall St's proprietary "Fair Ratio" of 14.87x.

feed.title: AvalonBay Communities Q1 FFO Softness Tests Bullish Narrative On Long Term Apartment Demand
feed.summary: AvalonBay Communities (AVB) reported Q1 2026 revenue of US$770.1 million and FFO of US$383.6 million, a slight decrease from Q1 2025. The company's net margin subtly declined, and a significant one-off gain of US$460.8 million influenced reported earnings, masking an underlying forecast decline of 11.1% per year for the next three years. Despite trading below its industry average P/E and having a substantial DCF fair value, the mixed financial signals and debt coverage issues present a complex picture for investors.

feed.title: Is It Time To Reassess AdaptHealth (AHCO) After A 60% One Year Rally?
feed.summary: AdaptHealth (AHCO) has seen a significant 60% rally over the past year, prompting a reassessment of its current valuation. A Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests the stock is undervalued by 64.0%, while its P/S ratio indicates a modest discount compared to a proprietary fair ratio. The article also presents bullish and bearish narratives, with fair values ranging from US$9.50 to US$17.00 per share, offering investors different perspectives on the company's future.

feed.title: NRG Energy Debt Tender Reshapes Covenants And Investor Risk Profile
feed.summary: NRG Energy's subsidiary, Lightning Power, has successfully completed a cash offer for its 7.250% senior secured notes, receiving over 99% early tenders. This move includes securing consent to amend the notes indenture, thereby removing restrictive covenants and releasing certain guarantees and collateral. This strategic debt restructuring aims to adjust NRG's balance sheet and future obligations in a single transaction, influencing its financial flexibility and interest costs going forward.

feed.title: Alexandria Real Estate Equities (ARE) Sees Q1 FFO Surge Questioning Bearish Cash Flow Narratives
feed.summary: Alexandria Real Estate Equities (ARE) reported a significant surge in Q1 2026 funds from operations (FFO) to US$636.9 million, despite trailing 12-month net income losses of US$1.1 billion. This strong FFO performance supports the bullish argument that the company's long-duration life science leases and large campus investments can generate substantial cash flow even amid accounting pressures and declining revenue. However, the stock trades below analyst targets and its DCF fair value, reflecting investor concerns over persistent losses and interest expenses.

feed.title: Trash and the Hunger for Power: How Waste Management, Zefiro Methane, and NextEra Energy Are Doing Good - and Making Money
feed.summary: This article examines how Waste Management, Zefiro Methane, and NextEra Energy are tackling environmental challenges while profitably expanding their businesses. Waste Management is diversifying into medical waste disposal, Zefiro Methane is addressing abandoned oil and gas wells, and NextEra Energy is meeting the high energy demands of the AI industry with sustainable solutions. These companies demonstrate how economic activity can align with environmental responsibility, providing stable and growth-oriented investment opportunities.

feed.title: Akebia (NASDAQ: AKBA) plans major increase in authorized common shares
feed.summary: Akebia Therapeutics is seeking shareholder approval to significantly increase its authorized common shares from 350 million to 500 million, and total capital stock from 375 million to 525 million, at its virtual 2026 annual meeting on June 17, 2026. This move aims to provide the company with greater flexibility for future financing, strategic deals, and equity incentives without requiring further charter amendments. Shareholders will also vote on director elections, executive compensation, its frequency, and the ratification of Ernst & Young LLP as the independent auditor.

feed.title: NetApp collabs with Google Cloud for AI-driven operations
feed.summary: NetApp is deepening its collaboration with Google Cloud by adopting Google Cloud AI technologies and Gemini Enterprise internally. This move aims to leverage AI for enhanced productivity in product development and sales operations, while also helping NetApp's customers integrate AI with their data. The partnership emphasizes delivering AI-driven insights to accelerate development cycles and provide more relevant solutions to customers, with NetApp acting as an "AI practitioner."

feed.title: Addex GABAB PAM Candidate Demonstrates Solid Anti-Tussive Activity in Bleomycin IPF-Related Chronic Cough Model
feed.summary: Addex Therapeutics announced promising preclinical data for its GABAB positive allosteric modulator (PAM) candidate, showing significant anti-tussive activity in a bleomycin-induced idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis (IPF) exacerbated chronic cough model. The compound demonstrated robust and sustained reductions in cough frequency, improved lung pathology, and maintained a favorable safety profile. This supports its potential as a novel and well-tolerated treatment for chronic cough, addressing a significant unmet medical need, particularly in IPF patients where current treatments like baclofen have limiting side effects.

feed.title: Nordic stocks in North America - Autoliv rose 0.6 percent
feed.summary: This article reports on the performance of Nordic stocks trading in North America. Autoliv, an automotive safety company, saw its stock rise by 0.6 percent. Other Nordic companies like Nokia, Meren Energy, and Novo Nordisk also experienced varying changes in their stock prices.

feed.title: Boeing and LightSolver Form Strategic Financial Partnership to Develop Laser-Based Accelerator for Large-Scale Engineering Simulation
feed.summary: Boeing has formed a strategic financial partnership with LightSolver to develop a laser-based accelerator for large-scale engineering simulations. This collaboration aims to improve the performance, scalability, and energy efficiency of complex physics-based modeling, particularly for degradation-driven structural effects. Boeing is funding the optimization of LightSolver's Laser Processing Unit™ (LPU) to enhance simulation fidelity and reduce computational costs across critical engineering challenges.

feed.title: EEOC sues Alto Ingredients for allegedly axing electrician over disability
feed.summary: The U.S. Equal Employment Opportunity Commission (EEOC) has sued Alto Ingredients for allegedly terminating an electrician due to his disability, a violation of the Americans with Disabilities Act. A recent ruling revealed challenges in the litigation, including an unprepared EEOC investigator, but the court denied Alto's requests for additional documents. The case is moving towards a decision on the core discrimination claim.

feed.title: Manager sues TJX, says he was fired after refusing to downgrade older workers
feed.summary: A former TJX Companies store manager, Jean Velez, has filed a lawsuit alleging he was fired after refusing to downgrade older employees' performance reviews and reporting age discrimination. Velez claims he faced transfer, increased scrutiny, and a disciplinary investigation shortly after raising these concerns. The lawsuit invokes federal age and disability discrimination acts, as well as the Florida Civil Rights Act.

feed.title: Delta Air Lines defeats pilots' USERRA claims in federal appeal
feed.summary: Delta Air Lines won a federal appeal against two former pilot-reservists who claimed they were constructively terminated due to their military service and denied proper pension contributions and vacation accrual. The Eleventh Circuit Court of Appeals sided with Delta, finding that while military status played a role in the pilots' treatment, the airline would have taken the same actions due to the pilots' abuse of sick leave benefits. The court also affirmed Delta's methods for pension contributions and found the pilots' vacation leave comparison invalid.

feed.title: American Express Trades Below 20 P/E — Is Buffett’s Second-Largest Bet Now a Steal?
feed.summary: American Express (AXP) shares have dropped 15% this year, pushing its price-to-earnings ratio below 20. Despite this, the company reported strong first-quarter revenue growth of 11% and an 18% surge in diluted earnings per share, with luxury spending by card members rising 18%. While management did not raise full-year guidance, long-term investors may see this as a strategic capital allocation for sustained growth rather than a dampened short-term outlook.

feed.title: Vanguard Capital Management (NYSE: WCN) reports 19.9M shares, 7.78%
feed.summary: Vanguard Capital Management has reported a beneficial ownership of 19,908,434 shares of Waste Connections Inc. common stock, which accounts for 7.78% of the class as of March 31, 2026. The filing indicates sole voting power for over 4 million shares and sole dispositive power for all 19.9 million shares. This disclosure, a Schedule 13G, was signed by Ashley Grim, Head of Global Fund Administration, on April 28, 2026, and provides transparency on Vanguard's significant stake in the company.

feed.title: vTv Therapeutics (NASDAQ: VTVT) details 2026 virtual annual meeting, board and pay
feed.summary: vTv Therapeutics is holding its 2026 annual virtual-only meeting on June 10, 2026, where stockholders will vote on electing seven directors and ratifying Ernst & Young LLP as the independent auditor. The proxy statement details the company's governance structure, board composition, director compensation, and executive compensation for 2025, including CEO Paul Sekhri's $6.64 million total compensation and CFO Michael Tung's $2.44 million. Key investors, including MacAndrews & Forbes and several institutional investors, hold significant stakes and have board nomination rights.

feed.title: Australia’s Woodside Sees Higher LNG Prices Boosting Results
feed.summary: Woodside Energy Group Ltd. anticipates increased earnings in upcoming quarters, driven by higher liquefied natural gas (LNG) prices due to contract time lags. The company's average realized price for LNG rose 11% in the first quarter, while global oil and gas benchmarks saw significant jumps, influenced by the Iran war.

feed.title: Vista Energy B de C : Voting results (afEJoMBOoF08xZTP Vista Summaryoftheresolutionsadoptedbytheshareholders)
feed.summary: Vista Energy, S.A.B. de C.V. held its Annual Ordinary General Shareholders' Meeting on April 28, 2026, where shareholders approved several key resolutions. These included ratifying a capital stock reduction to absorb losses, approving the CEO's and Board of Directors' reports for 2025, and establishing a compensation plan for board members for 2026. Shareholders also approved a maximum fund of US$150 million for share repurchases for the 2026 fiscal year.

feed.title: Executive pay, equity plan and auditor on ballot at INNOVATE Corp (VATE) 2026 meeting
feed.summary: INNOVATE Corp (VATE) will hold its 2026 annual shareholder meeting virtually on June 11, 2026, where stockholders will vote on electing four directors, an advisory vote on executive compensation, an increase in shares for the Omnibus Equity Award Plan, and ratifying BDO USA, P.C. as auditor. The board recommends voting "FOR" all proposals. The company also reported 2025 business highlights, including significant revenue growth for DBM Global and key product approvals for MediBeacon.

feed.title: Cooper Companies Stock Plummets 9.9% With 7-Day Losing Streak
feed.summary: Cooper Companies (NYSE: COO) stock has experienced a significant downturn, plummeting 9.9% and extending its losing streak to seven consecutive days. This decline indicates a challenging period for the company's market performance.

feed.title: Ex-PM accuses Franklin Templeton of disguising retaliation as a layoff
feed.summary: A former Franklin Templeton portfolio manager, Jordan Strah, is suing the company, alleging he was fired under the guise of a reduction in force after reporting alleged trading fraud by his manager. Strah claims he faced retaliation, including a negative performance review and subsequent termination, after escalating his concerns about data manipulation to company leadership and eventually to Franklin Resources' general counsel. The lawsuit, filed in the Southern District of New York, accuses Franklin Templeton of disguising his termination as a layoff.

feed.title: Worker says ADM ignored her assault report, then fired her in retaliation
feed.summary: A lawsuit has been filed against Archer Daniels Midland (ADM) by a 24-year veteran, Reyna Medina, alleging that the company ignored her report of a workplace assault and subsequently fired her in retaliation. Medina claims she experienced gender-based discrimination after a promotion, culminating in an unaddressed physical shove by a coworker and her eventual termination, followed by alleged sabotage of a new job opportunity. ADM has not yet responded to the allegations.

feed.title: Ventas expects 2026 senior living investments will total $3B by year’s end
feed.summary: Ventas, a Chicago-based REIT, anticipates its senior living investments will reach $3 billion by the end of 2026, driven by growing investor interest and demographic shifts. The company has already closed $1.7 billion in investments this year and plans to continue its '1-2-3' strategy to expand its senior housing operating portfolio. Ventas reported strong first-quarter growth with a more than 15% increase in SHOP same-store net operating income.

feed.title: Former Brinks Employee Sentenced to Federal Prison in Robbery Scheme
feed.summary: A former Brink's Inc. employee, Isaiah Warren, has been sentenced to 171 months in federal prison for robbing two armored trucks and ordered to pay $696,700 in restitution. Warren, 25, used a firearm in both incidents, one in November 2024 involving $34,700, and another a month later where he stole approximately $662,000. He was apprehended in January 2025 during a traffic stop, found with a firearm used in the robberies, marijuana, and stolen cash.

feed.title: bioAffinity Technologies Launches New Study To Expand Use of CyPath® Lung Technology for Asthma and COPD Diagnosis and Treatment
feed.summary: bioAffinity Technologies is partnering with Brooke Army Medical Center (BAMC) on a new pilot study to expand the use of its CyPath® Lung technology for diagnosing and treating asthma and COPD. The study aims to identify specific inflammatory biomarkers in sputum samples to help personalize treatment plans and monitor drug effectiveness for these debilitating respiratory diseases. This collaboration leverages bioAffinity's proprietary flow cytometry+AI technology, with the goal of shifting toward a precision medicine approach for asthma and COPD management.

feed.title: PennyMac Mortgage Investment Trust credit profile centers on mortgage REIT operations
feed.summary: PennyMac Mortgage Investment Trust (PMT) operates as a mortgage REIT through its operating partnership and subsidiaries, focusing on residential loan production, interest rate strategies, and credit-sensitive investments. The company's business model encompasses a diversified approach to the U.S. mortgage finance market, including investments in mortgage servicing rights and various structured products. Its credit strategies involve credit risk transfer agreements linked to correspondent production, providing broad sector exposure.

feed.title: Roundhill Bitcoin Covered Call Strategy ETF announces weekly distribution of $0.1454
feed.summary: The Roundhill Bitcoin Covered Call Strategy ETF has declared a weekly distribution of $0.1454 per share. This announcement details the distribution for its shareholders.

feed.title: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Ltd. (TSM.US) Fully Sells Down Stake in Arm Holdings plc (ARM.US), Cashes In Over USD200M
feed.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Ltd. (TSM.US), through its subsidiary TSMC Partners, has fully divested its stake in Arm Holdings plc (ARM.US). The company sold 1.11 million Arm shares between April 28 and 29 for approximately USD231 million, having initially invested as a cornerstone investor in 2023 for about USD100 million. This divestment comes as part of TSM's strategy to dispose of equity investments, with Arm's shares experiencing a significant drop recently.

feed.title: Toll Brothers to Aquire Buffington Homes of Arkansas
feed.summary: Toll Brothers announced its plan to acquire Buffington Homes of Arkansas, a luxury home builder based in Fayetteville. This acquisition will give Toll Brothers an immediate presence in the rapidly growing Fayetteville and Bentonville markets. Buffington Homes' leadership and employees will join Toll Brothers, ensuring continuity and aiming to accelerate growth in Northwest Arkansas.

feed.title: Whirlpool Plans $60 Million Ohio Facility After $300 Million Manufacturing Expansion
feed.summary: Whirlpool Corp. is investing over $60 million to convert an existing facility in Perrysburg, Ohio, into a manufacturing site for appliance components, creating 100 to 150 jobs. This move aims to strengthen its domestic production network and improve supply chain efficiency, building on a previous $300 million expansion of its laundry manufacturing capacity. The company emphasizes its commitment to U.S. manufacturing and its confidence in American workers to produce high-quality, innovative products.

feed.title: Short Interest in Intuit Inc. (NASDAQ:INTU) Expands By 36.7%
feed.summary: Intuit Inc. (NASDAQ:INTU) experienced a significant 36.7% increase in short interest during April, reaching 9,419,775 shares as of April 15th, with a days-to-cover ratio of 2.5 days. The company recently beat quarterly earnings estimates and declared a quarterly dividend of $1.20 per share. Institutional ownership remains high at 83.66%, despite some recent analyst price target reductions.

feed.title: FactSet Research Systems Inc. (NYSE:FDS) Sees Significant Growth in Short Interest
feed.summary: FactSet Research Systems Inc. (NYSE:FDS) experienced a notable increase in short interest in April, rising 34.5% to 4,778,159 shares, representing 13.3% of the stock. Despite beating March-quarter earnings estimates with EPS of $4.46 and revenue of $611.02 million, analyst sentiment has cooled, leading to a "Reduce" consensus rating and a target price of $279.85. The company announced a quarterly dividend of $1.10 per share and boasts significant institutional ownership.

feed.title: Multiple Class A Common sales reported by holders (NASDAQ: TEM)
feed.summary: A Form 144 filing for Tempus AI, Inc. (NASDAQ: TEM) indicates proposed sales of Class A Common shares by various holders, including Eric Lefkofsky, Blue Media, LLC, and Gray Media, LLC. The sales occurred on February 18, 2026, February 19, 2026, and March 26, 2026, with significant share quantities and corresponding dollar proceeds reported. J.P. Morgan Securities LLC is listed as the broker for these transactions.

feed.title: Omnicom (OMC) Q1 2026 Earnings Transcript
feed.summary: Omnicom (OMC) reported strong Q1 2026 results, demonstrating 6.7% total and 3.9% organic revenue growth in its core operations, alongside a significant 240-basis-point increase in adjusted EBITDA margin, following its acquisition of Interpublic. The company is actively executing a $5 billion share repurchase plan and strategically divesting non-core assets to enhance margin quality and focus on high-growth sectors like integrated media. Omnicom also highlighted the successful deployment and scaling of its AI-powered Omni platform, which is showing tangible benefits in client performance and operational efficiency.

feed.title: Tarsus (NASDAQ: TARS) outlines 2026 proxy votes and executive pay plans
feed.summary: Tarsus Pharmaceuticals, Inc. (NASDAQ: TARS) has outlined its plans for the 2026 Annual Meeting, where stockholders will vote on the election of four Class III directors, an advisory approval of executive compensation, and the ratification of Ernst & Young LLP as the independent auditor. The company reported strong commercial performance for its product XDEMVY in 2025, with net product sales reaching $451.4 million. Tarsus details its executive compensation philosophy, which includes performance-based incentives and long-term equity awards, and highlights its corporate governance standards.

feed.title: Sun Communities (SUI) Q1 FFO Of US$121.6 Million Tests Stable Cash Flow Narrative
feed.summary: Sun Communities (SUI) reported Q1 2026 revenue of US$512.1 million and Funds From Operations (FFO) of US$121.6 million, but a basic EPS loss of US$0.07. Despite the stable cash flow narrative supported by FFO and predictable income from manufactured housing and RV sites, the company remains unprofitable with a trailing 12-month net income loss of US$60.5 million. Bearish concerns about widening losses and interest coverage pressure are noted, while bullish arguments highlight the stock's discount to fair value and projected earnings growth.

feed.title: RSUs listed for sale by issuer via Goldman Sachs (SSSS) — Form 144
feed.summary: A Form 144 notice has been filed by Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC regarding proposed sales of restricted stock units (RSUs) acquired as compensation from Suro Capital Corp (SSSS). The filing details specific grant dates and unit amounts, including 6,082 units acquired on May 31, 2024, and 4,548 units acquired on June 05, 2025. It lists Goldman Sachs as the broker, but does not specify aggregate proceeds, offering mechanics, or timing of completed sales for these proposed transactions.

feed.title: What To Expect From Labcorp’s (LH) Q1 Earnings
feed.summary: Labcorp (NYSE: LH) is scheduled to report its Q1 earnings this Thursday before market open. The healthcare diagnostics company missed revenue expectations last quarter but did beat full-year EPS guidance. While analysts expect revenue to grow 4.8% year-on-year, past performance shows Labcorp has missed Wall Street’s revenue estimates multiple times in the last two years.
